figures.caption: Figure 1: Our proposed Conditional LoRA Steering (CoLoRS) mechanism to steer any vector given a conditioning parameter.
Figure 3: Accuracy-efficiency comparison on the DVD benchmark. Marker size is proportional to the number of parameters.

Figure 2: Overview of CoLoRSMamba. (a) Full architecture: The video backbone processes the video input X𝑣 , while audio backbone processes the log-mel spectrogram of X𝑎 in parallel. At each layer ℓ , the VideoMamba CLS token CLS𝑣ℓ conditions AudioMamba via a Conditional LoRA Steering module. The final video and audio descriptors (z𝑣 , z𝑎) are concatenated and fed to a binary classifier, while calculating a symmetric AV-InfoNCE loss aligning the two modalities. (b) Bidirectional Mamba block shared by both backbones.

Table 9: Qualitative examples from the DVD prediction flip analysis. The top two rows show Audio Helps cases for 2 violent labeled clips. . The bottom two rows show Audio Hurts cases for 2 violent labeled clips.

Table 1: Comparison across audio-only, video-only, and multimodal baselines on the audio-filtered NTU-CCTV and DVD splits. In the Modality column, A = audio-only, V = video-only, and A/V = audio-video. In the Architecture column, Transf. = Transformer.
